mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding supply capacity and economic strength, leading to higher long-term interest rates as investors anticipate stronger growth and inflationary pressure over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the 20-day forward yield does not show a positive median return after INDPRO increases in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.5% signals tightening labor market conditions, which typically leads to higher wage inflation expectations and pushes Treasury yields upward over a short horizon of 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% within a one-month period
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 6.5%, the 5-day forward yield declines more often than not (sign test p > 0.05) in at least 50% of triggering episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer and housing demand, which together boost GDP growth expectations and push Treasury yields higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal (both HOUST and RSAFS rising) leads to yield decreases in more than 40% of cases over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero (below 0.25%), a rise in the CPI signals imminent inflationary pressure, causing the market to anticipate a tightening cycle, which lifts intermediate and long-end Treasury yields over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between 10-year yield and fed funds does not widen by at least 10 bps in the 20 days following a CPI increase of >0.2% while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects overall economic expansion, which typically leads to higher Treasury yields as investors demand higher compensation for inflation and growth risk over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward yield is lower after GDPC1 increases in more than 50% of instances (binomial test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the start of a tightening cycle, which directly raises short-term rates and spills over into higher long-term yields via term premium adjustments over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a FEDFUNDS increase above 0.25%, the 20-day yield change is negative in the majority of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals synchronized labor and manufacturing expansion, which strongly raises growth expectations and pushes yields higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint increase of PAYEMS and INDPRO does not result in a median positive yield change over 60 days in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals a recovery in residential investment, which boosts growth expectations and leads to higher Treasury yields as the risk of deflation recedes over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6% and HOUST increases, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than half of observations over a 3-year walk-forward period.
